data.reasoningText:
Now let me build the project to see the actual error, and understand the issue.

The project has a target `GenerateSampleCode` that runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates a `.cs` file into the intermediate output path. However, the generated file is not being added to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using `<ItemGroup>` with `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />`.

Let me first build to confirm the error.

arguments.description: Build the project to see the error

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-92c5cf5734fd4f359ca31e9bd87ee1f9 && dotnet build 2>&1 | tail -20", "description": "Build the project to see the error", "initial_wait": 30}

data.content:
The file is generated successfully but never added to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the `.cs` file but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the intermediate output directory but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild generated the file before compilation, but the C# compiler had no idea it existed.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
